keyPoints: A 2:1 multiplexer selects one of three input bits to pass to the output.
A 2:1 multiplexer has two input lines (S and A), one output line (Y), and three input select lines.
To implement a 2:1 multiplexer in Verilog, use the following logic: SELECT = S; Y = Y when SELECT = 1, Y = A when SELECT = 0.
A 3:1 multiplexer selects one of four input bits to pass to the output.
A 3:1 multiplexer has three input lines (S0, S1, A), one output line (Y), and four input select lines.
To implement a 3:1 multiplexer in Verilog, use the following logic: SELECT0 = S0; SELECT1 = S1; SELECT = SELECT0 + SELECT1; Y = Y when SELECT = 0 or 1, Y = A when SELECT = 2.
